_meta.purpose: Single source-of-truth mapping effect/zone/palette names to binary IDs for the Thomas helmet light show system. Includes machine-readable definitions used by the scene compiler AND human-readable annotations for AI agents authoring scenes. The compiler reads `id`, `type`, `range`, and `zones` fields. AI agents read everything including `description`, `when_to_use`, `when_not_to_use`, `notes`, and `examples`.
companion_docs: sd_card_animation_architecture.md — binary bundle format and ESP-NOW protocol

_authoring_guidance_for_ai_agents.overview: When asked to draft a scene for a Daft Punk song, an AI agent should: (1) consult the song's structure (intro, verse, chorus, bridge, outro) and beat grid; (2) for each segment, pick a palette that matches the section's energy and aesthetic; (3) within each segment, place cues that translate the music's character to light — beat-driven effects on rhythmic sections, breathing/sustained effects on atmospheric sections, sprites/text/VU on the matrix during high-information moments; (4) prefer fewer cues with longer durations (let effects play out) over many short cues; (5) mirror by default — use `BOTH_EARS` and `BOTH_VISORS` instead of explicit left/right unless asymmetry is intentional.
common_authoring_mistakes: Authoring per-beat cues when a single beat-aware effect would do the same thing more cleanly. Use EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_GRADIENT for a whole chorus, not 64 separate brightness cues.
Forgetting that matrix brightness is capped at 45/255 in firmware — authoring matrix params at 200 just wastes parameter space (engine will clamp).
Ignoring the connector LED defaults — they already provide a baseline gold beat flash + rainbow ring. Override only when needed.
Mixing palettes mid-segment for no clear musical reason. Pick a palette per segment, stick with it, change at segment boundaries.
Using EFFECT_FULL_FLASH too often. It's a punctuation effect, not a base layer.

thomas_hardware_notes.ear_diffuser: Thomas helmet ear hardware note: the ear LEDs sit behind a diffuser panel used for edge lighting. All 16 LEDs blend into a single perceived color — gradient detail is invisible. Use EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_SOLID for beat-driven sections (uniform color, whole-ear flash on beat). EFFECT_EAR_BREATHE for atmospheric sections. EFFECT_EAR_SOLID for static holds. Do NOT use EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_GRADIENT, EFFECT_EAR_EKG_PULSE, or EFFECT_EAR_GRADIENT on Thomas — those are for Guy's helmet where the diamond zones are individually visible.

PAL_DISCOVERY.description: The workhorse palette. Helmet gold, Stella pink (magenta), Octave cyan, and a deep blue base. Inspired by Discovery-era Daft Punk visuals and the Crescendolls helmet sketch.
PAL_DISCOVERY.when_to_use: Default choice for most Discovery-era tracks (Crescendolls, Aerodynamic groove sections, Digital Love, Harder Better Faster Stronger). Energetic but not aggressive.
PAL_DISCOVERY.when_not_to_use: Quiet ballad-like passages (use PAL_OCEAN or PAL_INTRO_WARM). Aggressive breakdowns (use PAL_RED_BREAKDOWN). Cool nighttime atmospheric (use PAL_OCEAN).
PAL_DISCOVERY.notes: Tested on real WS2812 LEDs in the Crescendolls helmet sketch. These RGB values are known to look right on hardware.

PAL_FIRE.description: Black → red → orange → yellow → white. Classic fire ramp.
PAL_FIRE.when_to_use: Energy peaks, climactic moments, sections that escalate (e.g. a building chorus, a climax). Works well as a brightness ramp — gradient_index can represent intensity.
PAL_FIRE.when_not_to_use: Most Discovery-era tracks (too aggressive). Calm passages. Mirroring scenes that need cool tones.
PAL_FIRE.notes: Untested on hardware. May need yellow/white tuning after first real-LED testing.

PAL_OCEAN.description: Black → deep blue → cyan → light blue → white. Cool, atmospheric.
PAL_OCEAN.when_to_use: Atmospheric passages, calm bridges, outros that fade to peace. Pairs well with EFFECT_AMBIENT_BREATHE.
PAL_OCEAN.when_not_to_use: High-energy beat-driven sections. Anything that wants warmth.
PAL_OCEAN.notes: Untested on hardware. Blue channel is heavily attenuated by white-point correction (0.65×), so RGB values here should look like the corrected output.

PAL_FOREST.description: Black → dark green → green → yellow-green → cream. Earthy, organic.
PAL_FOREST.when_to_use: Reserved palette slot. No strong Discovery-era use case yet. Plausibly useful for cover-y/folkier tracks or for visual variety.
PAL_FOREST.when_not_to_use: Most Daft Punk material — they don't really do 'earthy.'
PAL_FOREST.notes: Untested on hardware. May be removed in future versions if no clear use emerges.

PAL_NEON.description: Hot pink, cyan, lime, yellow, purple. High-saturation pop colors with no neutrals.
PAL_NEON.when_to_use: Energetic, playful sections. Crescendolls' more whimsical bars. Faster, brighter Daft Punk material.
PAL_NEON.when_not_to_use: Atmospheric or moody sections. Anything that wants a sense of darkness or depth (no black/dark in the gradient).

PAL_GOLD_WARM.description: Helmet gold variations. Dim gold → mid gold → bright gold → amber → cream. No cool tones.
PAL_GOLD_WARM.when_to_use: Thomas-themed moments (his helmet IS gold). Solo gold-focused sections without competing colors. Vintage / classic Daft Punk visual quotes.
PAL_GOLD_WARM.when_not_to_use: When you need contrast — this palette is all warm tones, no opposites.
PAL_GOLD_WARM.notes: Helmet gold RGB (255, 184, 0) is sourced from the Crescendolls helmet sketch — tested on real LEDs.

PAL_COOL_SWEEP.description: Cool-to-warm sweep used in Aerodynamic groove sections. Deep blue → cyan → warm gold.
PAL_COOL_SWEEP.when_to_use: Aerodynamic-style groove sections — the song that explicitly uses this palette in the original sketch. Sections where you want gradual warming or cooling within a single cue.
PAL_COOL_SWEEP.when_not_to_use: Beat-driven sections where the palette needs hard transitions (use PAL_DISCOVERY instead).
PAL_COOL_SWEEP.notes: Closely matches CRGB(0, 24, 96) and CRGB(255, 192, 32) from the Aerodynamic groove renderer.

PAL_RED_BREAKDOWN.description: Aggressive red. Black gaps for tension. Used in Aerodynamic's break section.
PAL_RED_BREAKDOWN.when_to_use: Breakdown sections, ominous moments, tension building. Aerodynamic's break (60.7–89.6s in the original sketch).
PAL_RED_BREAKDOWN.when_not_to_use: Most of any song — this is a punctuation palette. Sustained use becomes overwhelming.
PAL_RED_BREAKDOWN.notes: Includes black at index 0 so gradient-based effects can create gaps/dropouts. Tested on hardware via the original Aerodynamic break sketch.

PAL_INTRO_WARM.description: Warm amber/orange for intros. Slow burn, calm energy.
PAL_INTRO_WARM.when_to_use: Slow openings, song intros, sections that build from quiet to energetic. Aerodynamic's intro (0–13.6s in the original sketch). Pairs naturally with EFFECT_EAR_BREATHE.
PAL_INTRO_WARM.when_not_to_use: Once a song has 'kicked in' — intro_warm is a setup color, not a payoff color.
PAL_INTRO_WARM.notes: RGB (192, 64, 8) is sourced from the original Aerodynamic intro sketch — tested on real LEDs.

PAL_MONO_WHITE.description: Pure grayscale. Black → gray → white. No hue.
PAL_MONO_WHITE.when_to_use: When you want pure brightness modulation without color (effect of a strobe or breathing without hue). Stark visual contrast moments.
PAL_MONO_WHITE.when_not_to_use: Almost any sustained section — too clinical. White hits hard on the eyes at full brightness.

PAL_MAGENTA_CYAN.description: Just magenta and cyan with hard transitions. Crescendolls' beat-cycle colors.
PAL_MAGENTA_CYAN.when_to_use: When a scene needs hard binary color transitions tied to beats — magenta on beat 1, cyan on beat 3 patterns (Crescendolls Segment A2 in the original sketch).
PAL_MAGENTA_CYAN.when_not_to_use: When smooth color blending is wanted. The gradient_stops here are intentionally flat-then-jump.
PAL_MAGENTA_CYAN.notes: Magenta and cyan are the exact Stella/Octave colors from the Crescendolls helmet. Hardware-tested.

PAL_RESERVED_11.description: Reserved. Likely future PAL_HUMAN_AFTER_ALL (stark black/red/white). Not yet defined.
PAL_RESERVED_11.when_to_use: Do not use in scenes — referencing this palette will fail validation.

PAL_RESERVED_12.description: Reserved. Likely future PAL_RAM (sunset oranges + electric blue). Not yet defined.
PAL_RESERVED_12.when_to_use: Do not use in scenes — referencing this palette will fail validation.

LEFT_EAR.description: Left ear diamond board, 16 LEDs in an EKG-like profile.
LEFT_EAR.when_to_use_directly: When intentionally creating asymmetric scenes where the two ears differ. Otherwise prefer BOTH_EARS.

LEFT_VISOR.description: Left visor edge board, 53 LEDs chained behind LEFT_EAR on pin 19. The 5×8 grid is the main rendering region; beat-flash trio and connector LEDs have default behaviors.
LEFT_VISOR.when_to_use_directly: When creating asymmetric scenes. Otherwise prefer BOTH_VISORS.

RIGHT_EAR.description: Right ear diamond board, 16 LEDs. Use only for explicitly asymmetric content.

RIGHT_VISOR.description: Right visor edge board, 53 LEDs chained behind RIGHT_EAR on pin 21. Col-to-band mapping is reversed vs left so that bass frequencies show on inner columns of both visors.

MATRIX.description: Front-of-helmet 32×8 matrix. Thomas's 'main display' zone. Carries text, VU meters, sprites. Strict brightness ceiling of 45/255 enforced in firmware.
MATRIX.when_to_use_directly: Always — matrix has no mirror partner. All matrix content goes here.

BOTH_EARS.description: Virtual zone. A cue targeting BOTH_EARS renders identically on both ears. Preferred over separate LEFT_EAR + RIGHT_EAR cues for mirrored content.

BOTH_VISORS.description: Virtual zone. Renders to both visors with column reversal applied to the right side, so the same logical content appears on both.

LEFT_CONNECTORS.description: Override target for the connector LEDs on the left visor. By default, the trio runs a gold beat flash and the ring runs a slow rainbow cycle — without any cue targeting this zone.

RIGHT_CONNECTORS.description: Right-side connector LEDs. Same defaults as LEFT_CONNECTORS.

EFFECT_OFF.description: Turn all LEDs in the target zone to black. No parameters.
EFFECT_OFF.when_to_use: Use as a segment's base_effect when nothing should render outside of explicit cues. Use as the base_effect for an intro segment that begins in darkness. Use in a cue to explicitly silence a zone in the middle of a busy scene.
EFFECT_OFF.when_not_to_use: Avoid as the global base_effect for a whole scene — usually you want something subtle like a dim breathing palette instead.

examples.context: Explicitly black out the matrix mid-segment before a new text cue enters
examples.notes: No palette or params needed. Useful when a previous cue ends early (duration_ms expired) and you want explicit black rather than the segment base_effect filling the gap.

params.name: color_index
params.description: Position 0–255 into the active palette gradient.

params.name: brightness
params.description: Scales the palette color. Subject to EAR_VISOR_BRIGHTNESS_MAX (185) in firmware.

EFFECT_EAR_SOLID.description: Fill the ear with a single solid color from the palette.
EFFECT_EAR_SOLID.when_to_use: As a static base layer during sustained passages. As a 'state indicator' showing which segment is active. For ear-LED moments where simplicity is right.
EFFECT_EAR_SOLID.when_not_to_use: Anywhere you want movement or beat-awareness. Pair with a more dynamic visor/matrix effect for contrast.

examples.context: A held magenta ear during a sustained chorus
examples.notes: Palette index 192 hits Stella pink in PAL_DISCOVERY. Brightness 160 is well under the 185 ceiling.

params.name: bottom_color
params.description: Color at BOT_PEAK (index 0). Renders through LOWER, FLAT, UPPER blending to top color.

params.name: top_color
params.description: Color at TOP_PEAK (index 15).

EFFECT_EAR_GRADIENT.description: Vertical gradient on the diamond. BOT_PEAK → LOWER → FLAT → UPPER → TOP_PEAK fades smoothly from bottom_color to top_color.
EFFECT_EAR_GRADIENT.when_to_use: Default ear-rendering effect for most segments. Sets up a held color palette that the matrix and visor can punctuate against.
EFFECT_EAR_GRADIENT.when_not_to_use: When beat-driven brightness modulation is wanted (use EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_GRADIENT instead). When a single solid color is enough (use EFFECT_EAR_SOLID).

examples.context: Warm bottom-to-bright-top gradient during a verse
examples.notes: Lit ember at the bottom of the diamond, brighter amber rising to TOP_PEAK. Sustained energy without motion.

params.name: decay_steepness
params.description: 0 = no decay (flat), 180 = tight beat-locked decay, 255 = very sharp. Match to music feel.

params.name: bottom_color
params.description: Static color at BOT_PEAK.

params.name: top_brightness
params.description: Peak brightness of the beat-cycled top color.

params.name: downbeat_flash
params.description: 0 = disabled. Otherwise peak white-flash brightness on TOP_PEAK on beat 1 of each bar.

params.name: subbeat_sparkle
params.description: 0 = disabled. Otherwise white sparkle brightness on BOT_PEAK at half-beat intervals.

EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_GRADIENT.description: The Crescendolls workhorse. Vertical gradient where the top color cycles through palette gradient indices in sync with the beat grid, with a brightness envelope that decays each beat. Optional downbeat flash on TOP_PEAK and sub-beat sparkle on BOT_PEAK add per-beat punctuation.
EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_GRADIENT.when_to_use: High-energy beat-driven sections — choruses, drops, dance-floor moments. Pairs beautifully with PAL_DISCOVERY where the beat-cycling shows off all four palette colors over each bar.
EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_GRADIENT.when_not_to_use: Quiet/atmospheric sections (use EFFECT_EAR_BREATHE). When the music has no clear 4-on-the-floor beat. When the beat grid in the scene header is unreliable.
EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_GRADIENT.notes: Reads the beat grid from the scene header. One full beat-color-cycle traversal takes 4 beats (one bar).

examples.context: Crescendolls chorus — full energy, downbeat-emphasized
examples.notes: Deep indigo at the base (palette pos 0), beat-cycled bright colors at top, strong downbeat flash. The sparkle gives a hi-hat feel even between beats.

params.name: breath_period_100ms
params.description: Breath period in 100ms units. 40 = 4 seconds per breath. Typical 30–60.

params.name: min_brightness
params.description: Brightness at trough of breath.

params.name: max_brightness
params.description: Brightness at peak of breath.

EFFECT_EAR_BREATHE.description: Held vertical gradient that fades up and down on a slow sinusoidal cycle. The 'meditative' effect.
EFFECT_EAR_BREATHE.when_to_use: Intros, outros, atmospheric breakdowns, sustained passages where calm is wanted. Pairs with PAL_INTRO_WARM or PAL_OCEAN.
EFFECT_EAR_BREATHE.when_not_to_use: Beat-driven sections. Anything that needs urgency or rhythm.

examples.context: Aerodynamic-style intro breath
examples.notes: 4-second breath, deep amber low to amber high. Restrained max brightness (130 of 185 ceiling) keeps the intro from competing with whatever the matrix is doing.

params.name: beats_per_pulse
params.description: 1 = one pulse per beat, 2 = per half-note, 4 = per measure

params.name: base_brightness
params.description: Dim base color for non-pulse LEDs.

EFFECT_EAR_EKG_PULSE.description: A bright pulse travels through the diamond: BOT_PEAK → LOWER → FLAT → UPPER → TOP_PEAK and back. Anchored to the beat grid.
EFFECT_EAR_EKG_PULSE.when_to_use: When the ear should feel 'alive' with rhythmic motion. Distinct from EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_GRADIENT — that fills the whole ear with each beat color, this is a discrete pulse traveling through.
EFFECT_EAR_EKG_PULSE.when_not_to_use: When the visor is already doing a lot of motion — the ear pulse might compete. Quiet sections. NOT SUITABLE FOR THOMAS HELMET: the ears sit behind a diffuser for edge lighting — individual LED pulse patterns are invisible through diffuser material. Use EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_SOLID instead for Thomas helmet — it produces a uniform whole-zone brightness pulse with no gradient, which is exactly what reads through a diffuser. EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_GRADIENT is appropriate for Guy's helmet where the diamond zones are individually visible.

examples.context: Aerodynamic breakdown — ear pulses with the beat, blood-red wave traveling BOT_PEAK to TOP_PEAK
examples.notes: pulse_color 192 = bright_red in PAL_RED_BREAKDOWN. beats_per_pulse 1 = one traversal per beat. pulse_width_leds 2 keeps the pulse tight and crisp. tail_decay 180 fades fast — each pulse is gone before the next fires. base_brightness 20 holds a dim floor between pulses.

params.name: color
params.description: Single color applied uniformly to all 16 ear LEDs. All LEDs are identical — no gradient.

params.name: peak_brightness
params.description: Brightness at the beat peak. Subject to EAR_VISOR_BRIGHTNESS_MAX (185) in firmware. Recommended range 125–185 when ears are behind a diffuser.

params.name: decay_steepness
params.description: How fast brightness falls after each beat peak. 0 = no decay (held flat), 180 = tight beat-locked decay, 255 = instant cut to zero. Match to music feel.

params.name: downbeat_flash
params.description: 0 = disabled. Otherwise, a uniform white-flash overlay applied to all LEDs on beat 1 of each bar. Adds a stronger visual punch on the downbeat vs the regular beat flashes.

EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_SOLID.description: Uniform single-color beat flash. All 16 ear LEDs are the same color and brightness at all times — no gradient, no zone differentiation. The whole ear pulses as one unit in sync with the beat grid, with a brightness envelope that peaks on each beat and decays between beats. Designed for ears behind a diffuser where gradient detail is invisible.
EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_SOLID.when_to_use: Thomas helmet in all beat-driven sections. Primary ear effect for the Thomas helmet since the diffuser makes gradient detail invisible. Looks like a single large LED pulsing with the music — clean, punchy, and readable at distance.
EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_SOLID.when_not_to_use: Guy helmet (use EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_GRADIENT there — LEDs are fully visible). Atmospheric sections without a clear beat (use EFFECT_EAR_BREATHE instead). Any context where color variation per LED zone is desired.
EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_SOLID.notes: The companion to EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_GRADIENT — same beat-sync behavior, no gradient. EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_GRADIENT remains the correct choice for Guy's helmet where the diamond zones are individually visible.

examples.context: One More Time chorus — whole ear flashes gold on every beat, strong downbeat emphasis
examples.notes: color 128 = helmet_gold in PAL_DISCOVERY. peak_brightness 185 is the EAR_VISOR_BRIGHTNESS_MAX ceiling — all 16 LEDs hit max on every beat. decay_steepness 180 gives a tight decay that clears before the next beat, keeping each hit punchy. downbeat_flash 220 adds a white boost on beat 1 of each bar for emphasis.

examples.context: Aerodynamic solo section — dim red uniform pulse, building intensity
examples.notes: No downbeat_flash during the building tension phase — the regular beat pulse is enough. Raise peak_brightness and add downbeat_flash at the climax moment.

EFFECT_VISOR_SOLID.description: Fill the 5×8 grid with one solid color from the palette. Connector LEDs run their defaults (or are addressed separately).
EFFECT_VISOR_SOLID.when_to_use: As a base layer during sustained moments. As a contrast to a more dynamic ear effect.

examples.context: Crescendolls chorus — held cyan visor while the ears do the rhythmic beat work
examples.notes: color_index 64 = octave_cyan in PAL_DISCOVERY. Intentionally modest brightness (100 of 185 ceiling) — the visor is a static color field the ear effects play against.

EFFECT_VISOR_GRADIENT_V.description: Vertical gradient on the 5×8 grid. Row 0 (bottom) = bottom_color, Row 7 (top) = top_color.
EFFECT_VISOR_GRADIENT_V.when_to_use: Setting up the visor as a held background. Pairs well with a beat-driven ear effect — keep visor static, ear dynamic.

examples.context: One More Time verse — dark indigo at the bottom, helmet gold warming toward the top, held static
examples.notes: bottom_color 0 = deep_indigo, top_color 128 = helmet_gold in PAL_DISCOVERY. Low bottom brightness (20) grounds the visor; top brightness (140) drives the warmth. Row 0 is physical bottom of the visor grid, row 7 is top.

params.name: inner_color
params.description: Color at col 1 (inner edge of visor).

params.name: outer_color
params.description: Color at col 5 (outer edge of visor).

EFFECT_VISOR_GRADIENT_H.description: Horizontal gradient. When mirrored to right visor, col mapping is reversed so 'inner edge = inner edge' semantic is preserved on both sides.
EFFECT_VISOR_GRADIENT_H.when_to_use: Cool moments where the helmet should have a directional color — e.g. cooler tones toward the center, warmer toward the edges.

examples.context: Aerodynamic groove — cool deep blue near the face, warming to gold toward the outer visor edge
examples.notes: inner_color 0 = deep_blue in PAL_COOL_SWEEP at col 1 (nearest face). outer_color 220 = warm_gold at col 5 (outer edge). The engine automatically reverses col mapping on RIGHT_VISOR so the inner=cool, outer=warm semantic is preserved on both sides.

params.name: vu_name
params.description: Name of a VU sequence in the scene's resources.vu_sequences array. Compiler resolves to ID. Must be a 5-band sequence.

params.name: single_color
params.description: Used only when bar_color_mode is single_color.

params.name: bg_color
params.description: Used only when bg_fill_mode is palette_color.

EFFECT_VISOR_VU_PRERENDERED.description: Render a pre-rendered 5-band VU meter on the visor grid. Each band corresponds to a column. Heights animate per the sequence's frame data.
EFFECT_VISOR_VU_PRERENDERED.when_to_use: When the visor should respond to the music's frequency content directly. Choruses, build-ups, sections where the energy spread across frequencies tells the story.
EFFECT_VISOR_VU_PRERENDERED.when_not_to_use: When VU data is not authored for this segment. When you want the visor to do something more spatial than just frequency bars.

examples.context: Crescendolls chorus — 5 frequency bands pumping across visor columns, colors drawn from the active palette
examples.notes: bar_color_mode per_band_palette maps each of the 5 columns to a different position in PAL_DISCOVERY — bass column hits helmet_gold, treble hits cyan, etc. single_color is ignored in this mode. bg_fill_mode black keeps unlit LEDs off for maximum bar contrast. vu_name must match a band_count:5 entry in resources.vu_sequences.

EFFECT_VISOR_BEAT_WIPE.description: A color wave travels across the grid in sync with the beat. Picture: every beat (or every N beats), a column/row lights up bright then the wave moves on.
EFFECT_VISOR_BEAT_WIPE.when_to_use: Beat-driven sections where you want directional motion across the visor. Pairs well with a static ear effect.
EFFECT_VISOR_BEAT_WIPE.when_not_to_use: Mid-energy sections where motion would be too much. Outro/intro (use breathing instead).

examples.context: Aerodynamic groove section — a cyan wave wipes bottom-to-top across the visor on every beat
examples.notes: direction bottom_to_top sweeps upward through rows 0→7. beats_per_wipe 1 = one wipe per beat, tight and energetic. wave_color 160 = cyan in PAL_COOL_SWEEP. wave_width 2 is a narrow front — increase to 3 or 4 for a softer trailing edge. bg_brightness 30 leaves the non-wave area faintly lit. For a slower, more majestic feel use beats_per_wipe 4.

params.name: rotation_period_beats
params.description: Beats per full rotation. 16 = 4 bars per rotation.

EFFECT_VISOR_HALO_ROTATE.description: A bright spot rotates around the perimeter of the 5×8 grid over a configurable period. Maintains a sense of motion at a slower-than-beat pace.
EFFECT_VISOR_HALO_ROTATE.when_to_use: Sustained groove sections where you want continuous slow motion without hard rhythmic emphasis. Crescendolls' Segment B (locked-in groove) used this pattern.
EFFECT_VISOR_HALO_ROTATE.when_not_to_use: Short segments — the rotation won't complete. Highly rhythmic moments (use BEAT_WIPE instead).

examples.context: Crescendolls locked-groove segment — a magenta spot drifts slowly around the visor perimeter over 4 bars
examples.notes: halo_color 192 = stella_pink in PAL_DISCOVERY. rotation_period_beats 16 = one full perimeter rotation per 4 bars at 120 BPM — slow enough to read as drift rather than spinning. halo_width 4 gives a soft smear; reduce to 3 for a tighter spot. floor_brightness 25 keeps the rest of the grid faintly lit.

params.name: density
params.description: LEDs sparkled per second on average.

EFFECT_VISOR_SPARKLE.description: Random LEDs in the visor grid briefly light up and decay. Hash-based deterministic randomness — same scene time produces same sparkle pattern, reproducibly.
EFFECT_VISOR_SPARKLE.when_to_use: High-frequency hi-hat/cymbal moments. Building energy. Sections that need 'shimmer' on top of a held base.
EFFECT_VISOR_SPARKLE.when_not_to_use: When clarity matters — sparkle is distracting under text or sprites. NOT SUITABLE FOR THOMAS HELMET: individual LED sparkle points are lost through the ear diffuser material and produce an uneven muddy result. Appropriate for Guy's helmet only where LEDs are directly visible.

examples.context: HBFS verse — sparse white sparkles over a near-dark visor, suggesting hi-hat texture
examples.notes: sparkle_color 255 = white (top of PAL_MONO_WHITE). density 80 = roughly 80 LEDs/sec across the 40-LED grid, about 2 sparks per frame at 60 fps — present but not overwhelming. decay_rate 200 fades quickly so each spark reads as crisp. bg_brightness 15 is near-black floor.

params.name: brightness
params.description: Hard-clamped to 45/255 in firmware regardless of authored value.

EFFECT_MATRIX_SOLID.description: Fill the entire matrix with one solid color.
EFFECT_MATRIX_SOLID.when_to_use: Almost never as the primary matrix content. Useful as a brief flash, a state indicator, or a fallback during sections you haven't designed yet.

examples.context: HBFS drop — matrix flashes gold for one beat before handing off to VU bars
examples.notes: color_index 128 = helmet_gold in PAL_DISCOVERY. brightness 45 is the MATRIX_BRIGHTNESS_MAX ceiling — authoring anything higher is silently clamped by firmware. duration_ms 500 = one beat at 120 BPM before the next matrix cue takes over.

params.name: vu_name
params.description: Name of a 32-band VU sequence in resources.

EFFECT_MATRIX_VU_PRERENDERED.description: 32-band pre-rendered VU meter on the matrix. Each matrix column is one frequency band.
EFFECT_MATRIX_VU_PRERENDERED.when_to_use: Choruses, drops, sections where the music's frequency spread is the main visual story. The matrix's VU is the most impressive thing it can do.
EFFECT_MATRIX_VU_PRERENDERED.when_not_to_use: Quiet sections (VU bars on near-silence look limp). Sections where you want the matrix doing text or sprites. Long full-song VU sequences are size-prohibitive (a 32-band 20 fps full song = 150 KB+); use VU only during segments where it really sings.

examples.context: Crescendolls main chorus — 32-band rainbow VU filling the full matrix width
examples.notes: bar_color_mode rainbow_per_col sweeps the 32 columns through the full hue wheel — bass left through treble right. single_color is ignored. bar_brightness 40 is just under the 45 ceiling. vu_name must match a band_count:32 entry in resources.vu_sequences. Limit 32-band VU to specific segments, not full-song, to stay under the 64 KB scene RAM ceiling.

params.name: sprite_name
params.description: Name of a sprite in resources.sprites.

params.name: fps_override
params.description: 0 = use sprite's authored fps.

EFFECT_MATRIX_SPRITE.description: Play a sprite (small region animation) on the matrix at a fixed position. Sprites are stored in the scene's sprite library.
EFFECT_MATRIX_SPRITE.when_to_use: Signature visual moments — a spinning globe for 'Around the World,' a rotating record, a small animated icon during a recognizable hook. One-of-a-kind matrix content that VU and text can't capture.
EFFECT_MATRIX_SPRITE.when_not_to_use: Frequently — sprites are size-expensive (24 KB ceiling for all sprites in a scene combined). Use sparingly for impact.

examples.context: Crescendolls hook — a small spinning record loops on the right half of the matrix while text scrolls left
examples.notes: x_offset 20 places an 8-wide sprite at columns 20–27, leaving columns 0–19 free for a simultaneous scrolling text cue. y_offset 0 pins it to the top row. loop_mode loop keeps it spinning for the full cue duration. fps_override 0 uses the sprite's authored fps. brightness 35 is safely under the matrix ceiling of 45.

params.name: text
params.description: Name of a string in resources.text_strings.

params.name: x_position
params.description: Negative starts text off-screen left, useful for scroll-in.

EFFECT_MATRIX_TEXT.description: Render text from the scene's text_strings array on the matrix. The glyph subset is auto-computed by the compiler from the strings array.
EFFECT_MATRIX_TEXT.when_to_use: Song-recognition moments — title flashes, key lyric callouts. HBFS's 'HARDER BETTER FASTER STRONGER' scrolling text is the canonical use case. Daft Punk songs often have minimal lyrics, so text is a high-impact element.
EFFECT_MATRIX_TEXT.when_not_to_use: Constantly — text everywhere becomes noise. Reserve for moments that genuinely benefit from words.

examples.context: Scroll 'HARDER' across the matrix during a vocal hit
examples.notes: Starts text off-screen-left (-24), scrolls right at slow speed, lasts 3 seconds — enough for the word to cross the matrix. Color 128 = helmet gold in PAL_DISCOVERY.

params.name: scroll_speed
params.description: Pixels per second.

params.name: pattern_period
params.description: Pattern period in pixels (e.g., 4 = stripes 4 LEDs wide).

EFFECT_MATRIX_SCROLL_BG.description: Scrolling background pattern. Useful as a base under text or sprites.
EFFECT_MATRIX_SCROLL_BG.when_to_use: When the matrix needs visual texture under foreground elements. Long-form sustained sections where 'something is happening' without big rhythmic moments.
EFFECT_MATRIX_SCROLL_BG.when_not_to_use: Alone — the patterns are too geometric to be the main visual. Always layer foreground elements (text, sprites) on top via additive flag.

examples.context: HBFS verse — diagonal gold/indigo stripe pattern scrolls slowly beneath HARDER BETTER text
examples.notes: pattern diagonal at 45°. color_a 128 = helmet_gold in PAL_DISCOVERY, color_b 0 = deep_indigo. scroll_speed 12 pixels/sec is slow enough to read as texture rather than motion. brightness 20 keeps the background clearly subordinate to text layered on top via additive flag. pattern_period 4 = stripes 4 LEDs wide. Never use this effect alone — always layer foreground content on top.

params.name: density
params.description: Number of simultaneously active stars. 8–20 is typical. Higher values fill the matrix more densely.

params.name: twinkle_speed
params.description: Sine-wave cycle speed for each star's brightness. 20 = slow dreamy twinkle, 80 = lively shimmer, 150+ = rapid sparkle.

params.name: max_brightness
params.description: Peak brightness of each star at the top of its sine cycle. Subject to MATRIX_BRIGHTNESS_MAX=45 firmware cap. 35–45 recommended.

params.name: color_mode
params.description: 0=white, 1=warm_white (slight amber), 2=random_per_star (each star picks a random hue at spawn), 3=palette_sweep (stars cycle through the active palette). Default 0 for classic starfield.

params.name: drift_speed
params.description: Slow horizontal drift of the star field. 0=stationary, 1–30=subtle parallax, higher=visible scroll. Used to simulate motion through space.

EFFECT_MATRIX_STARFIELD.description: Procedural twinkling starfield across the full 32×8 matrix. Stars are placed at random positions and breathe in/out using independent sine-wave oscillators at slightly different phases and speeds, producing a living sky effect. Stars respawn at new random positions when their cycle completes (density is maintained). No pre-rendered sprite data needed — effect runs entirely in firmware. Inspired by the star layer in the Around the World Arduino sketch.
EFFECT_MATRIX_STARFIELD.when_to_use: Atmospheric intros, breakdowns, quiet verses, or any moment needing a 'space' or 'cosmic' backdrop. Excellent underneath a simultaneously playing EFFECT_MATRIX_TEXT cue (layer with additive flag) so the text rides on a starfield. Works in nearly every Daft Punk song given the Interstella 5555 space aesthetic.
EFFECT_MATRIX_STARFIELD.when_not_to_use: High-energy peak sections where something more dynamic (VU, strobes, cylon) better matches the energy. Don't layer with EFFECT_MATRIX_SPRITE unless the sprite leaves blank columns for stars to show through.
notes: density 12, twinkle_speed 35, max_brightness 40, color_mode 0, drift_speed 0 is a safe conservative starting point.
For around-the-world: density 14 matches the 14 hardcoded stars in the reference sketch.
color_mode 2 (random per star) gives a festive/Crescendolls feel. color_mode 0 (white) is cinematic/space.
Combining drift_speed 8 + color_mode 1 (warm_white) creates a cozy, nostalgic feel for slower songs.
Can run simultaneously with EFFECT_MATRIX_TEXT using additive compositing — text pixels override stars, stars fill the blank space.

examples.context: Atmospheric intro before the Around the World drop — white starfield, slow twinkle
examples.notes: density 14 matches the 14 stars in the Around the World reference sketch. twinkle_speed 30 is a slow dreamy twinkle. max_brightness 40 is safely under the matrix ceiling of 45. color_mode 0 = white stars for a classic space look. drift_speed 4 adds subtle motion without looking like a scroll.

examples.context: Crescendolls breakdown — colorful starfield while ears breathe slowly
examples.notes: density 20 gives a dense festive feel. color_mode 2 assigns each star a random hue — colorful and energetic. twinkle_speed 50 is lively but not frantic. drift_speed 0 keeps the field stationary so it reads as a backdrop, not a scroll effect.

params.name: color
params.description: Palette index for the leading bar and trail. Use a vivid mid-range palette color for maximum visual impact.

params.name: bar_width
params.description: Width of the bright leading bar in LEDs. 1=single column laser, 2=tight bar, 3–4=classic cylon width. Applies in the sweep direction.

params.name: trail_length
params.description: Number of columns behind the bar that fade exponentially from bar brightness down to zero. 0=no trail (hard edge), 4=subtle glow, 8=long comet tail, 12+=dramatic sweep.

params.name: speed
params.description: Sweep speed in LEDs per frame tick. 10=slow scan, 40=moderate, 80=fast bounce, 120+=very fast strobe-like. At beat_sync=1 this is overridden by BPM.

params.name: brightness
params.description: Peak brightness of the leading bar. Subject to MATRIX_BRIGHTNESS_MAX=45 cap. 35–45 recommended for the bar; trail fades below this.

params.name: direction
params.description: 0=horizontal sweep (left→right→left across 32 columns, full 8-row height). 1=vertical sweep (top→bottom→top across 8 rows, full 32-column width).

params.name: beat_sync
params.description: 0=free-running at speed param. 1=one full sweep per bar (left-to-right on bar 1, right-to-left on bar 2). 2=one half-sweep per beat (reaches far edge on each downbeat). Overrides speed param when nonzero.

EFFECT_MATRIX_CYLON.description: Classic sweeping scanner bar with exponential glow trail, bouncing left-right (or top-bottom) across the matrix. The leading bar is at peak brightness; the trail decays exponentially over trail_length columns behind it. At the far edges the bar reverses direction, creating the iconic Cylon/KITT bounce. Supports free-running speed or beat-locked timing. Fills the full matrix height (horizontal mode) or width (vertical mode) for maximum visual impact.
EFFECT_MATRIX_CYLON.when_to_use: High-energy verses and choruses with a driving beat — especially songs with a strong left-right rhythmic feel. Works as a standalone effect or layered under EFFECT_MATRIX_TEXT with additive compositing. Beat-sync mode makes the sweep feel locked to the music. Red (PAL_WARM red stop) is the classic Cylon color; yellow-gold fits Daft Punk's palette. Vertical mode adds variety mid-song.
EFFECT_MATRIX_CYLON.when_not_to_use: Quiet atmospheric sections (use EFFECT_MATRIX_STARFIELD or EFFECT_MATRIX_SCROLL_BG instead). Not ideal simultaneously with a sprite effect since both compete for horizontal space.
notes: For a 32-column matrix at beat_sync=1 and 120 BPM (500ms/beat, 2 beats/bar = 1000ms bar): the bar travels 32 columns in ~1000ms, which is ~32 columns/second = speed param ~32.
trail_length 6–8 with bar_width 2 is the classic KITT look. bar_width 1 + trail_length 10 is a comet.
direction 1 (vertical) on an 8-row matrix is a very fast sweep — use lower speed values (5–15) to keep it readable.
Pairing a red cylon sweep (beat_sync=1) with EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_SOLID in matching red creates a unified visual that pulses as one system.

examples.context: Harder Better Faster Stronger verse — red cylon sweep locked to the bar
examples.notes: color 192 = a warm red stop in PAL_DISCOVERY. bar_width 2 + trail_length 8 is the classic KITT comet look. beat_sync 1 locks one full bounce per bar so the sweep lands on the downbeat every time. brightness 42 is just under the matrix ceiling for full visual impact.

examples.context: Aerodynamic solo — fast gold vertical sweep with long trail
examples.notes: color 128 = helmet_gold in PAL_DISCOVERY. direction 1 = vertical sweep on the 8-row axis. beat_sync 2 = half-sweep per beat snaps the top→bottom → bottom→top rhythm to every downbeat. bar_width 1 + trail_length 5 gives a sharp laser-thin vertical sweep.

params.name: color
params.description: Palette index for the expanding ring. Bright saturated colors read best against the dark matrix background.

params.name: brightness
params.description: Peak brightness of the ring at the moment it fires. Subject to MATRIX_BRIGHTNESS_MAX=45. Rings fade as they expand if fade_out is enabled.

params.name: speed
params.description: Expansion rate in pixels per frame tick. 8=slow pulse, 20=moderate, 40=fast burst. At beat_trigger=1, speed controls how quickly the fired ring travels outward before it vanishes — too slow and rings stack up; too fast and they're gone before you see them. 15–25 is the sweet spot for beat-locked radiating.

params.name: ring_thickness
params.description: Width of the ring wavefront in pixels. 1=razor thin, 2=solid ring, 3–4=fat band. Thicker rings are more visible on the sparse matrix.

params.name: ring_count
params.description: Number of concentric rings simultaneously in flight. Each subsequent ring is spaced ring_spacing pixels behind the leading ring, creating a ripple effect. 1=single pulse, 2–3=ripple pool, 4=dense ripple.

params.name: ring_spacing
params.description: Distance in pixels between the leading edges of simultaneous rings. Only meaningful when ring_count > 1. 4–6 gives tight ripples, 8–12 gives airy separated rings.

params.name: fade_out
params.description: 0=rings maintain brightness until they hit the edge and vanish abruptly. 1=rings fade linearly from peak_brightness at center down to 0 at max radius. Fade mode looks more organic; no-fade is punchy and sharp.

params.name: beat_trigger
params.description: 0=continuous looping: a new ring fires automatically as soon as the previous set reaches the edge. 1=beat-triggered: a new leading ring fires on each beat downbeat, up to ring_count rings in flight simultaneously. 2=bar-triggered: fires on bar 1 only, creating a single outward pulse per musical phrase.

EFFECT_MATRIX_RADIATE.description: Beat-synced concentric rings expanding outward from the center of the 32×8 matrix. On a 32×8 display the rings are elliptical — they reach the top/bottom edges (4 rows) well before the left/right edges (16 columns), producing a wide oval pulse. Each ring starts at peak brightness and optionally fades as it travels. Multiple rings can be in flight simultaneously for a ripple-pool effect. In beat_trigger mode a fresh ring fires on each downbeat, creating the visual equivalent of a bass drum hitting water.
EFFECT_MATRIX_RADIATE.when_to_use: Drops, chorus entries, downbeat punctuation moments. Especially powerful when paired with EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_SOLID so the ears and matrix fire together on each beat — the ear flashes and the ring expands simultaneously. Works well in slower, more atmospheric songs where the ring expansion is clearly visible between beats. beat_trigger=2 (bar-triggered) is useful for very fast songs where per-beat rings would stack up too fast.
EFFECT_MATRIX_RADIATE.when_not_to_use: Very fast tempos with beat_trigger=1 — rings won't clear before the next fires and the display becomes a blob. Not ideal simultaneously with EFFECT_MATRIX_CYLON since both dominate the full matrix surface. Use EFFECT_MATRIX_SCROLL_BG or EFFECT_MATRIX_STARFIELD as a backdrop layer instead.
notes: The 32×8 aspect ratio means rings are oval, not circular. Max radius top/bottom = 4px, left/right = 16px. Rings reach the edge at those distances.
ring_count 2, ring_spacing 6, beat_trigger 1, fade_out 1 is a good starting point for chorus sections.
At 120 BPM (500ms/beat) with speed=20, a ring travels 10px per beat-tick — it just clears the short axis (4px) in under one beat, which looks clean.
Combining with EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_SOLID (same color, same beat) creates a unified helmet-wide pulse that reads as one system: ears flash + rings radiate simultaneously.
beat_trigger=0 (continuous) is good for non-beat sections — just a calm, looping outward breathe on the matrix.

examples.context: One More Time chorus — cyan rings expanding on each beat alongside ear beat solid
examples.notes: color 64 = octave_cyan in PAL_DISCOVERY. beat_trigger 1 fires a new ring on each downbeat. ring_count 2 means two rings are in flight — the leading ring and one ripple behind it. ring_spacing 7 keeps them visually separated. fade_out 1 makes rings dissolve gracefully at the edges. Pair with EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_SOLID using the same cyan palette index for a unified pulse.

examples.context: Crescendolls breakdown — slow continuous gold radiate while ears breathe
examples.notes: color 128 = helmet_gold. beat_trigger 0 = continuous loop, no beat dependency. speed 8 is a slow meditative expansion. ring_count 3 + ring_spacing 5 creates a dense ripple-pool that fills the matrix with gentle motion. brightness 35 is soft enough to feel atmospheric rather than assertive.

params.name: color
params.description: Palette index for the rain drops and trails. Classic digital rain uses green (try PAL_NEON green stop). Gold suits Daft Punk's palette. Use color_mode=1 for randomized column hues.

params.name: density
params.description: Number of columns with an active falling drop at any moment. On a 32-column matrix: 4–8=sparse drizzle, 12–18=moderate rain, 24–32=downpour. Columns without a drop are dark.

params.name: speed
params.description: Fall speed in rows per frame tick. On an 8-row matrix: 5=slow drift (drop takes ~1.6s to cross), 20=medium (0.4s), 60=fast flash (0.13s). Higher values make drops look like strobes rather than falls.

params.name: trail_length
params.description: Number of rows fading behind the leading pixel. 0=single pixel dot (no trail), 2=short comet, 4=half-column fade, 7=full column fade from top. Trail decays exponentially: each row is ~50% the brightness of the row ahead.

params.name: brightness
params.description: Peak brightness of the leading pixel. Trail rows are exponentially dimmer. Subject to MATRIX_BRIGHTNESS_MAX=45.

params.name: color_mode
params.description: 0=uniform (all drops use color param). 1=random_column (each column gets a random palette color at spawn, held for its full fall). 2=gradient_fall (each drop starts at one palette end and shifts hue as it falls — dramatic on long trails).

params.name: spawn_mode
params.description: 0=staggered (each column spawns at a random delay after the previous drop clears, creating organic async rain). 1=synchronized (all active columns fall in lockstep — creates a waterfall/curtain feel rather than individual drops).

EFFECT_MATRIX_RAIN.description: Falling pixel drops with exponential glow trails cascade from the top to the bottom of the 32×8 matrix, column by column. Each active column has one leading bright pixel and a trail of fading rows above it. When a drop reaches row 7 (bottom) it clears and the column goes dark until a new drop spawns. In staggered mode columns fire independently for an organic rain look; in synchronized mode all active columns fall in lockstep for a curtain or waterfall effect. On a shallow 8-row matrix the drop transit time is short — fast speeds look like strobing columns, slow speeds look like drips. Best at moderate speed (15–30) with trail_length 3–5 to make the fall clearly readable.
EFFECT_MATRIX_RAIN.when_to_use: Energetic verses and breakdowns, particularly for songs with a mechanical or digital feel (Harder Better Faster Stronger, Technologic). Works as a standalone full-matrix effect or as a backdrop with EFFECT_MATRIX_TEXT layered on top (additive). density 8–14 fills the matrix without looking cluttered. Pairs well with EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_SOLID firing on the same beat for a unified system pulse.
EFFECT_MATRIX_RAIN.when_not_to_use: Very slow atmospheric sections where the constant motion feels restless (use EFFECT_MATRIX_STARFIELD instead). Avoid on sections where a sprite or text is the primary content — rain competes for every column. spawn_mode=1 (synchronized) can feel stiff for organic song sections; reserve it for robotic/mechanical parts.
notes: 8-row matrix means drops transit very quickly. speed 15–25 is the most visually readable range — you see the leading pixel and trail before it exits the bottom.

trail_length 4–5 fills most of the 8-row column, which looks dramatic on this shallow matrix. trail_length 7 = the whole column lights up and fades, which looks like a column flash more than a falling drop.
density 16 (half the columns) + spawn_mode 0 (staggered) is a good default that fills the matrix without looking like a solid block.
color_mode 1 (random per column) with PAL_NEON gives a festive multi-color downpour — good for high-energy sections. color_mode 0 + a single strong color is more focused and readable.
Layering EFFECT_MATRIX_TEXT on top with additive compositing lets title text float over the rain — cinematic.

examples.context: Harder Better Faster Stronger verse — fast gold rain, moderate density
examples.notes: color 128 = helmet_gold in PAL_DISCOVERY. density 16 = half the columns active at any time, staggered async. speed 22 gives a brisk fall that reads cleanly on 8 rows. trail_length 4 produces a half-column fade. brightness 40 is near the matrix ceiling for vivid leading pixels.

examples.context: Crescendolls intro — slow colorful drizzle before the groove kicks in
examples.notes: color_mode 1 = each column picks a random PAL_NEON color at spawn. density 8 is sparse enough to feel like an opening drizzle. speed 10 is slow and deliberate — you can watch each drop fall. trail_length 5 creates long fading tails that fill most of the column height.

params.name: color
params.description: Palette index for the iris fill. Bright saturated colors read best — cyan, gold, red, or magenta. The pupil center (if pupil_mode>0) is rendered darker automatically.

params.name: brightness
params.description: Peak iris brightness. Subject to MATRIX_BRIGHTNESS_MAX=45. 38–45 recommended — eyes need to be vivid to read as eyes rather than blobs.

params.name: blink_rate
params.description: Beats between blinks. 4=blinks every 4 beats (once per bar at 4/4), 8=every 2 bars, 16=very occasional. The blink closes and reopens within one beat regardless of this value.

params.name: blink_speed
params.description: How quickly the eyelid closes and reopens. 1=very slow dramatic blink (takes most of the beat), 5=natural blink, 10=snap blink (single frame). Slow blinks feel sleepy or ominous; snap blinks feel robotic.

params.name: look_mode
params.description: 0=forward stare (pupils fixed center — unsettling). 1=wander (pupils drift slowly left/right at random intervals, pausing at each position). 2=beat-snap (pupils shift to a new random position on each downbeat, locked to the music rhythm).

params.name: expression
params.description: 0=neutral oval (symmetrical open eye shape). 1=angry (top edge of each eye angles inward toward the nose — brow-furrowed look). 2=sleepy (top eyelid droops halfway, eyes are half-closed — heavy-lidded look). Expression changes the pixel mask used for the eye outline.

params.name: pupil_mode
params.description: 0=solid iris (entire eye filled with color — simple and bold). 1=dark pupil (center 2×2 area rendered at ~20% brightness creating iris+pupil separation). 2=beat-dilate (pupil contracts to 1 pixel on downbeat then dilates back to 2×2 — eerie pulsing effect synchronized to music).

EFFECT_MATRIX_EYES.description: Two procedurally animated eyes on the 32×8 matrix. Each eye is a ~10×6 pixel oval shape with an eyelid that blinks independently from a shared blink timer. The eyes are centered vertically (1-row margin top/bottom) and positioned symmetrically on the left and right halves of the matrix — left eye at approximately columns 4–13, right eye at approximately columns 18–27, with a 4-column gap between them. The pupils can drift or snap to look left/right within their socket. Blink animation collapses the eye vertically row-by-row from outside in, holds closed for one frame, then reopens. Combine with a slow EFFECT_EAR_BREATHE for an eerie staring presence effect.
EFFECT_MATRIX_EYES.when_to_use: Intros, breakdowns, dramatic pauses, any moment calling for a spooky or robotic stare. Works extremely well for slower Daft Punk tracks and atmospheric transitions. The helmet literally has a face — lean into it. beat_snap look_mode (2) makes the eyes feel alive and reactive during active sections. expression=1 (angry) during intense builds, expression=2 (sleepy) during slow breakdowns.
EFFECT_MATRIX_EYES.when_not_to_use: Very high-energy sections where faster effects (CYLON, RAIN, RADIATE) better match the energy. Don't layer with EFFECT_MATRIX_TEXT — the eyes and text compete for the same pixel real estate. Not suitable when a sprite is playing in the same column range.
notes: The left eye occupies columns 4–13, right eye columns 18–27. Columns 0–3, 14–17, and 28–31 are dark — giving a natural frame around the eyes.
blink_rate 4 (every bar) is the most natural feel. blink_rate 8 feels occasional and eerie. blink_rate 1 (every beat) looks like rapid mechanical blinking — very robotic.
look_mode 0 (unblinking forward stare) with expression 0 is deeply unsettling. Use intentionally.
pupil_mode 2 (beat-dilate) is subtle but effective — the pupil contraction lands on the kick drum, which reads as the eyes 'reacting' to the music.
blink_speed 10 (snap) + blink_rate 4 + look_mode 2 (beat-snap) creates a robotic, precise feel good for Harder Better Faster Stronger.
blink_speed 2 + look_mode 1 (wander) + expression 2 (sleepy) is perfect for slow breakdown sections.

examples.context: Crescendolls breakdown — eerie forward stare, cyan eyes, slow blink
examples.notes: color 64 = octave_cyan in PAL_DISCOVERY. blink_rate 8 = very infrequent blinking for an unsettling stare. blink_speed 3 = slow deliberate blink. look_mode 0 = fixed forward — no gaze drift. expression 0 = neutral oval. pupil_mode 1 = dark center adds iris/pupil detail. Pair with EFFECT_EAR_BREATHE using the same cyan for a calm, eerie atmosphere.

examples.context: Harder Better Faster Stronger chorus — robotic snap-blinking gold eyes locked to the beat
examples.notes: color 128 = helmet_gold. blink_rate 4 = one blink per bar. blink_speed 10 = snap blink (one frame) — robotic. look_mode 2 = pupils snap to new position on each downbeat. expression 1 = angry brow furrow matches the intensity of the track. pupil_mode 2 = beat-dilate pulses the pupil on every kick. Pair with EFFECT_EAR_BEAT_SOLID in matching gold.

params.name: peak_bright
params.description: Subject to per-zone caps in firmware.

params.name: decay_10ms
params.description: Decay duration in 10ms units. 20 = 200ms decay.

params.name: include_matrix
params.description: 0 = ears/visors only, 1 = include matrix (clamped to matrix ceiling).

EFFECT_FULL_FLASH.description: Bright flash across multiple zones at once. Punctuation effect for downbeat emphasis.
EFFECT_FULL_FLASH.when_to_use: Sparingly. Big downbeat moments, drops, climactic punctuation. The opening hit of a chorus.
EFFECT_FULL_FLASH.when_not_to_use: Repeatedly — flashes lose impact when overused. As a base effect. Sustained sections.

examples.context: One More Time chorus drop — full-helmet cyan flash on the first downbeat
examples.notes: color 64 = octave_cyan in PAL_DISCOVERY. peak_bright 255 hits maximum before per-zone firmware caps apply (ears 185, matrix 45). decay_10ms 25 = 250ms decay — half a beat at 120 BPM. include_matrix 1 fires all zones. Use at most once per section — repeated flashes lose impact fast.

EFFECT_AMBIENT_BREATHE.description: Slow synchronized breathing across all ear and visor zones (and matrix if zone-targeted). Cross-zone variant of EFFECT_EAR_BREATHE.
EFFECT_AMBIENT_BREATHE.when_to_use: Outros, sustained calm moments where the whole helmet should pulse together. Pairs with PAL_OCEAN or PAL_INTRO_WARM.

examples.context: One More Time outro — visors breathing slowly in cool blue as the song winds down
examples.notes: color 160 = light_blue in PAL_OCEAN. period_100ms 50 = 5-second breath — slow and restful. max_brightness 110 avoids harshness; the 185 ceiling is not the right target for a calm passage. Pair with a simultaneous EFFECT_EAR_BREATHE cue on BOTH_EARS using the same period so ears and visors breathe in sync.

EFFECT_CONN_SOLID.description: Override all 13 connector LEDs on one side with a single color, disabling the default beat-flash + rainbow behavior.
EFFECT_CONN_SOLID.when_to_use: When the scene wants the connectors silent or unified with the rest of the visor (e.g., 'everything goes red during the breakdown').
EFFECT_CONN_SOLID.when_not_to_use: When the defaults are doing what you want — defaults are usually fine.

examples.context: Aerodynamic breakdown — override connector defaults to uniform red so the whole visor edge reads as one color
examples.notes: color 192 = bright_red in PAL_RED_BREAKDOWN. Targeting LEFT_CONNECTORS with mirror_lr: true auto-mirrors to RIGHT_CONNECTORS. Replaces both the default gold beat-flash trio and the rainbow ring with uniform red. Set duration_ms to end before the next segment if you want the defaults to resume automatically.

EFFECT_CONN_BEAT_FLASH_OVERRIDE.description: Override the default gold beat flash on the connector trio with custom color/timing.
EFFECT_CONN_BEAT_FLASH_OVERRIDE.when_to_use: When the song calls for a different beat-flash color than gold (e.g., a red-themed segment where gold would clash).

examples.context: Crescendolls bridge — replace the default gold beat flash with cyan to match the PAL_MAGENTA_CYAN segment
examples.notes: flash_color 176 = octave_cyan_a in PAL_MAGENTA_CYAN. beats_between 1 = same cadence as the default gold flash, just color-swapped. apply_to trio_only leaves the 10-LED rainbow ring running its default cycle. To flash the entire 13-LED connector set instead, use apply_to full_set.

params.name: cycle_speed
params.description: 0.1 Hz units. 5 = 0.5 Hz (full cycle every 2 sec).

params.name: hue_step
params.description: Hue increment per connector LED. ~25 default.

EFFECT_CONN_RAINBOW_OVERRIDE.description: Adjust the default rainbow ring's speed/saturation/brightness without disabling it.
EFFECT_CONN_RAINBOW_OVERRIDE.when_to_use: Quick tweaks to make the rainbow feel right for a specific scene's energy (faster for chorus, slower for breakdown, etc.).

examples.context: One More Time intro — slow the default rainbow ring to a languid drift matching the song's pace
examples.notes: cycle_speed 2 = 0.2 Hz = one full rotation every 5 seconds, much slower than the default. hue_step 25 is the default LED-to-LED hue increment, giving a smooth multi-color ring. saturation 240 keeps colors vivid. This effect adjusts the existing rainbow; it does not disable the beat-flash trio.

complete_minimal_scene.description: A bare-minimum 30-second scene with one cue per zone. Useful as a template.
